perfectly\n233\t\n234\t---\n235\t\n236\t## Root Cause Analysis\n237\t\n238\t### Options Pipeline ZeroDivisionError\n239\t\n240\t**Technical Root Cause:**\n241\tThe `py_vollib_vectorized` library attempts to calculate implied volatility using the formula:\n242\t\n243\t```python\n244\t# /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/py_vollib_vectorized/implied_volatility.py, line 77\n245\tiv = vectorized_implied_volatility(undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag)\n246\t```\n247\t\n248\t**Division by Zero Conditions:**\n249\tThe calculation divides by one or more parameters that can be zero:\n250\t- `t` (time to expiration) = 0\n251\t- `F` (forward price) = 0  \n252\t- `K` (strike price) = 0\n253\t- Invalid option price input\n254\t\n255\t**Why This Occurs:**\n256\t1. **Upstream Data Quality**: Invalid options data reaches the calculation engine\n257\t2. **Missing Input Validation**: No parameter validation before mathematical operations\n258\t3. **Silent Failures**: Error causes pod restart but doesn't alert on data quality issues\n259\t\n260\t### IBKR MCP Error-Free Operation\n261\t\n262\t**Why No Errors Occur:**\n263\t1. **Robust Input Validation**: IBKR MCP validates all inputs before API calls\n264\t2. **Proper Error Handling**: Try-catch blocks prevent unhandled exceptions\n265\t3. **Session Management**: Authentication tokens properly refreshed and validated\n266\t4. **Graceful Degradation**: Network blips handled without application errors\n267\t\n268\t---\n269\t\n270\t## Recommendations\n271\t\n272\t### Immediate Actions Required 🔴\n273\t\n274\t#### 1. Fix Options Pipeline ZeroDivisionError\n275\t\n276\t**Priority:** CRITICAL — 42 occurrences in 30 days, active failures\n277\t\n278\t**Recommended Solution:**\n279\t\n280\t```python\n281\t# File: /app/app/app.py, function calculate_iv()\n282\tdef calculate_iv(chunk):\n283\t    \"\"\"Calculate implied volatility with input validation\"\"\"\n284\t    \n285\t    validated_rows = []\n286\t    \n287\t    for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n288\t        # Extract parameters\n289\t        t = row.get('T', 0)  # Time to expiration\n290\t        F = row.get('F', 0)  # Forward price  \n291\t        K = row.get('K', 0)  # Strike price\n292\t        option_price = row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0)\n293\t        flag = row.get('flag', 'C')\n294\t        \n295\t        # INPUT VALIDATION\n296\t        if t <= 0:\n297\t            logger.warning(\n298\t                f\"Invalid time parameter t={t} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n299\t                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n300\t            )\n301\t            continue\n302\t            \n303\t        if F <= 0:\n304\t            logger.warning(\n305\t                f\"Invalid forward price F={F} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n306\t                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"  \n307\t            )\n308\t            continue\n309\t            \n310\t        if K <= 0:\n311\t            logger.warning(\n312\t                f\"Invalid strike price K={K} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n313\t                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n314\t            )\n315\t            continue\n316\t            \n317\t        if option_price <= 0:\n318\t            logger.warning(\n319\t                f\"Invalid option price={option_price} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n320\t                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n321\t            )\n322\t            continue\n323\t        \n324\t        # SAFE CALCULATION WITH EXCEPTION HANDLING\n325\t        try:\n326\t            iv = py_vollib_vectorized.implied_volatility.vectorized_implied_volatility(\n327\t                undiscounted_option_price=option_price,\n328\t                F=F,\n329\t                K=K, \n330\t                t=t,\n331\t                flag=flag\n332\t            )\n333\t            row['iv'] = iv\n334\t            validated_rows.append(row)\n335\t            \n336\t        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n337\t            logger.error(\n338\t                f\"ZeroDivisionError for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: \"\n339\t                f\"price={option_price}, F={F}, K={K}, t={t}, flag={flag}\"\n340\t            )\n341\t            continue\n342\t            \n343\t        except Exception as e:\n344\t            logger.error(\n345\t                f\"Unexpected error calculating IV for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: {e}\"\n346\t            )\n347\t            continue\n348\t    \n349\t    return pd.DataFrame(validated_rows)\n350\t```\n351\t\n352\t**Implementation Steps:**\n353\t1. Add input validation before all mathematical operations\n354\t2. Wrap calculations in try-catch blocks\n355\t3. Log validation failures for data quality monitoring\n356\t4. Deploy to canary pod first, monitor for 24 hours\n357\t5. Roll out to all pods after validation\n358\t\n359\t#### 2. Monitor Pod Stability After Fix\n360\t\n361\t```bash\n362\t# Track restart reduction over time\n363\twatch -n 300 'kubectl --server=http://traefik-iad-options:8001 get pods -n options'\n364\t\n365\t# Monitor for ZeroDivisionError elimination  \n366\tkubectl --server=http://traefik-iad-options:8001 logs -f -n options \\\n367\t  options-greeks-7cbcd5dff4-24p6f -c worker | grep -i \"zerodivision\"\n368\t\n369\t# Track validation failure patterns\n370\tkubectl --server=http://traefik-iad-options:8001 logs -f -n options \\\n371\t  options-greeks-7cbcd5dff4-24p6f -c worker | grep \"Invalid.*parameter\"\n372\t```\n373\t\n374\t### Medium-Term Actions 🟡\n375\t\n376\t#### 3. Implement Data Quality Validation Layer\n377\t\n378\t**Purpose:** Prevent invalid data from reaching calculation engines\n379\t\n380\t```python\n381\tclass OptionsDataValidator:\n382\t    \"\"\"Validate options data quality before expensive calculations\"\"\"\n383\t    \n384\t    def __init__(self):\n385\t        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n386\t        self.validation_metrics = {\n387\t            'total_validated': 0,\n388\t            'validation_failures': {\n389\t                't_zero': 0,\n390\t                'f_invalid': 0, \n391\t                'k_invalid': 0,\n392\t                'price_invalid': 0\n393\t            }\n394\t        }\n395\t    \n396\t    def validate_row(self, row):\n397\t        \"\"\"Validate a single row of options data\"\"\"\n398\t        \n399\t        # Time to expiration must be positive\n400\t        if row.get('T', 0) <= 0:\n401\t            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['t_zero'] += 1\n402\t            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid T={row.get('T')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n403\t            return False\n404\t        \n405\t        # Forward price must be positive\n406\t        if row.get('F', 0) <= 0:\n407\t            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['f_invalid'] += 1\n408\t            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid F={row.get('F')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n409\t            return False\n410\t        \n411\t        # Strike price must be positive  \n412\t        if row.get('K', 0) <= 0:\n413\t            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['k_invalid'] += 1\n414\t            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid K={row.get('K')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n415\t            return False\n416\t        \n417\t        # Option price must be positive\n418\t        if row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0) <= 0:\n419\t            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['price_invalid'] += 1\n420\t            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid price={row.get('undiscounted_option_price')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n421\t            return False\n422\t        \n423\t        self.validation_metrics['total_validated'] += 1\n424\t        return True\n425\t    \n426\t    def get_metrics(self):\n427\t        \"\"\"Return validation statistics\"\"\"\n428\t        return self.validation_metrics\n429\t```\n430\t\n431\t#### 4. Add Observability and Telemetry\n432\t\n433\t**Prometheus Metrics:**\n434\t\n435\t```python\n436\tfrom prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram\n437\t\n438\t# Validation failure metrics\n439\tvalidation_failures = Counter(\n440\t    'options_validation_failures_total',\n441\t    'Total count of options data validation failures',\n442\t    ['reason']  # t_zero, f_invalid, k_invalid, price_invalid\n443\t)\n444\t\n445\t# Calculation success metrics  \n446\tcalculation_success = Counter(\n447\t    'options_calculation_success_total',\n448\t    'Total successful options calculations'\n449\t)\n450\t\n451\t# Calculation latency\n452\tcalculation_duration = Histogram(\n453\t    'options_calculation_duration_seconds',\n454\t    'Options calculation duration in seconds'\n455\t)\n456\t\n457\t# Pod restart tracking\n458\tpod_restarts = Counter(\n459\t    'options_pod_restarts_total',\n460\t    'Total options pod restarts',\n461\t    ['pod_name']\n462\t)\n463\t```\n464\t\n465\t**Grafana Dashboard Queries:**\n466\t\n467\t```promql\n468\t# Validation failure rate\n469\trate(options_validation_failures_total[5m])\n470\t\n471\t# Calculation success rate  \n472\trate(options_calculation_success_total[5m])\n473\t\n474\t# Pod restart frequency\n475\tincremt(options_pod_restarts_total[1h])\n476\t```\n477\t\n478\t### Long-Term Improvements 🟢\n479\t\n480\t#### 5. Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern\n481\t\n482\t**Purpose:** Prevent cascading failures when errors occur\n483\t\n484\t```python\n485\timport time\n486\tfrom enum import Enum\n487\t\n488\tclass CircuitState(Enum):\n489\t    CLOSED = \"closed\"    # Normal operation\n490\t    OPEN = \"open\"        # Failing, reject requests  \n491\t    HALF_OPEN = \"half_open\"  # Testing if recovery occurred\n492\t\n493\tclass OptionsCalculationCircuitBreaker:\n494\t    \"\"\"Circuit breaker for options calculations to prevent cascading failures\"\"\"\n495\t    \n496\t    def __init__(self, failure_threshold=10, timeout=300):\n497\t        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n498\t        self.timeout = timeout  # seconds to stay open\n499\t        self.failures = 0\n500\t        self.last_failure_time = None\n501\t        self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n502\t    \n503\t    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n504\t        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n505\t        \n506\t        # Check if circuit is open\n507\t        if self.state == CircuitState.OPEN:\n508\t            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n509\t                self.state = CircuitState.HALF_OPEN\n510\t                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker entering HALF_OPEN state\")\n511\t            else:\n512\t                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\n513\t                    f\"Circuit breaker is OPEN — {self.timeout - (time.time() - self.last_failure_time):.0f}s until retry\"\n514\t                )\n515\t        \n516\t        try:\n517\t            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n518\t            \n519\t            # Success in HALF_OPEN state -> close circuit\n520\t            if self.state == CircuitState.HALF_OPEN:\n521\t                self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n522\t                self.failures = 0\n523\t                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker closing after successful test\")\n524\t            \n525\t            return result\n526\t            \n527\t        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n528\t            self.failures += 1\n529\t            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n530\t            \n531\t            logger.error(f\"Calculation failure: {e}, total failures: {self.failures}\")\n532\t            \n533\t            # Open circuit if threshold exceeded\n534\t            if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:\n535\t                self.state = CircuitState.OPEN\n536\t                logger.critical(f\"Circuit breaker OPEN after {self.failures} failures\")\n537\t            \n538\t            raise\n539\t```\n540\t\n541\t#### 6. Enhanced Logging and Distributed Tracing\n542\t\n543\t**Structured Logging:**\n544\t\n545\t```python\n546\timport json\n547\timport logging\n548\t\n549\tclass StructuredLogger:\n550\t    \"\"\"JSON structured logging for better observability\"\"\"\n551\t    \n552\t    def __init__(self, service_name):\n553\t        self.service_name = service_name\n554\t        self.logger = logging.getLogger(service_name)\n555\t    \n556\t    def log_event(self, event_type, level, **kwargs):\n557\t        \"\"\"Log structured event\"\"\"\n558\t        log_entry = {\n559\t            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n560\t            'service': self.service_name,\n561\t            'event_type': event_type,\n562\t            'level': level,\n563\t            **kwargs\n564\t        }\n565\t        \n566\t        log_line = json.dumps(log_entry)\n567\t        \n568\t        if level == 'ERROR':\n569\t            self.logger.error(log_line)\n570\t        elif level == 'WARNING':\n571\t            self.logger.warning(log_line)\n572\t        else:\n573\t            self.logger.info(log_line)\n574\t\n575\t# Usage\n576\tlogger = StructuredLogger('options-pipeline')\n577\tlogger.log_event(\n578\t    event_type='calculation_failure',\n579\t    level='ERROR',\n580\t    symbol='AAPL',\n581\t    error='ZeroDivisionError',\n582\t    parameters={'t': 0, 'F': 150, 'K': 145}\n583\t)\n584\t```\n585\t\n586\t#### 7. Implement Data Quality Monitoring\n587\t\n588\t**Purpose:** Proactive detection of data quality issues\n589\t\n590\t```python\n591\tclass DataQualityMonitor:\n592\t    \"\"\"Monitor options data quality trends\"\"\"\n593\t    \n594\t    def __init__(self):\n595\t        self.quality_metrics = []\n596\t    \n597\t    def record_batch_quality(self, batch_data):\n598\t        \"\"\"Record quality metrics for a data batch\"\"\"\n599\t        total_rows = len(batch_data)\n600\t        invalid_rows = batch_data[\n601\t            (batch_data['T'] <= 0) | \n602\t            (batch_data['F'] <= 0) | \n603\t            (batch_data['K'] <= 0) |\n604\t            (batch_data['undiscounted_option_price'] <= 0)\n605\t        ]\n606\t        \n607\t        quality_score = (total_rows - len(invalid_rows)) / total_rows if total_rows > 0 else 0\n608\t        \n609\t        self.quality_metrics.append({\n610\t            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow(),\n611\t            'total_rows': total_rows,\n612\t            'invalid_rows': len(invalid_rows),\n613\t            'quality_score': quality_score,\n614\t            'symbols_affected': invalid_rows['symbol'].unique().tolist()\n615\t        })\n616\t    \n617\t    def get_quality_trend(self, hours=24):\n618\t        \"\"\"Get quality trend over specified hours\"\"\"\n619\t        cutoff = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=hours)\n620\t        recent_metrics = [m for m in self.quality_metrics if m['timestamp'] > cutoff]\n621\t        \n622\t        if not recent_metrics:\n623\t            return None\n624\t        \n625\t        avg_quality = sum(m['quality_score'] for m in recent_metrics) / len(recent_metrics)\n626\t        total_invalid = sum(m['invalid_rows'] for m in recent_metrics)\n627\t        \n628\t        return {\n629\t            'period_hours': hours,\n630\t            'average_quality_score': avg_quality,\n631\t            'total_invalid_rows': total_invalid,\n632\t            'batches_processed': len(recent_metrics)\n633\t        }\n634\t```\n635\t\n636\t---\n637\t\n638\t## Conclusions\n639\t\n640\t### System State **External APIs**: No rate limiting or 5xx errors in either system\n- **Authentication**: Both systems maintain successful authentication\n- **Infrastructure**: No cluster-level or node-level issues detected\n\n### Systemic Issues\n\n**Options Pipeline — Systemic Calculation Bug:**\n- **Nature**: Code-level mathematical error without input validation\n- **Reproducibility**: 100% — occurs consistently with invalid parameters\n- **Predictability**: High — occurs during IV calculations with bad data\n- **Scope**: Limited to options data processing, not gateway/auth\n\n**IBKR MCP — No Systemic Issues:**\n- **Nature**: No errors detected in any category\n- **Reproducibility**: N/A — no failures to reproduce\n- **Predictability**: N/A — no failure patterns\n- **Scope**: N/A — system operates perfectly\n\n---\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Options Pipeline ZeroDivisionError\n\n**Technical Root Cause:**\nThe `py_vollib_vectorized` library attempts to calculate implied volatility using the formula:\n\n```python\n# /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/py_vollib_vectorized/implied_volatility.py, line 77\niv = vectorized_implied_volatility(undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag)\n```\n\n**Division by Zero Conditions:**\nThe calculation divides by one or more parameters that can be zero:\n- `t` (time to expiration) = 0\n- `F` (forward price) = 0  \n- `K` (strike price) = 0\n- Invalid option price input\n\n**Why This Occurs:**\n1. **Upstream Data Quality**: Invalid options data reaches the calculation engine\n2. **Missing Input Validation**: No parameter validation before mathematical operations\n3. **Silent Failures**: Error causes pod restart but doesn't alert on data quality issues\n\n### IBKR MCP Error-Free Operation\n\n**Why No Errors Occur:**\n1. **Robust Input Validation**: IBKR MCP validates all inputs before API calls\n2. **Proper Error Handling**: Try-catch blocks prevent unhandled exceptions\n3. **Session Management**: Authentication tokens properly refreshed and validated\n4. **Graceful Degradation**: Network blips handled without application errors\n\n---\n\n## Recommendations\n\n### Immediate Actions Required 🔴\n\n#### 1. Fix Options Pipeline ZeroDivisionError\n\n**Priority:** CRITICAL — 42 occurrences in 30 days, active failures\n\n**Recommended Solution:**\n\n```python\n# File: /app/app/app.py, function calculate_iv()\ndef calculate_iv(chunk):\n    \"\"\"Calculate implied volatility with input validation\"\"\"\n    \n    validated_rows = []\n    \n    for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n        # Extract parameters\n        t = row.get('T', 0)  # Time to expiration\n        F = row.get('F', 0)  # Forward price  \n        K = row.get('K', 0)  # Strike price\n        option_price = row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0)\n        flag = row.get('flag', 'C')\n        \n        # INPUT VALIDATION\n        if t <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid time parameter t={t} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n            )\n            continue\n            \n        if F <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid forward price F={F} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"  \n            )\n            continue\n            \n        if K <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid strike price K={K} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n            )\n            continue\n            \n        if option_price <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid option price={option_price} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n            )\n            continue\n        \n        # SAFE CALCULATION WITH EXCEPTION HANDLING\n        try:\n            iv = py_vollib_vectorized.implied_volatility.vectorized_implied_volatility(\n                undiscounted_option_price=option_price,\n                F=F,\n                K=K, \n                t=t,\n                flag=flag\n            )\n            row['iv'] = iv\n            validated_rows.append(row)\n            \n        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"ZeroDivisionError for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: \"\n                f\"price={option_price}, F={F}, K={K}, t={t}, flag={flag}\"\n            )\n            continue\n            \n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"Unexpected error calculating IV for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: {e}\"\n            )\n            continue\n    \n    return pd.DataFrame(validated_rows)\n```\n\n**Implementation Steps:**\n1. Add input validation before all mathematical operations\n2. Wrap calculations in try-catch blocks\n3. Log validation failures for data quality monitoring\n4. Deploy to canary pod first, monitor for 24 hours\n5. Roll out to all pods after validation\n\n#### 2. Monitor Pod Stability After Fix\n\n```bash\n# Track restart reduction over time\nwatch -n 300 'kubectl --server=http://traefik-iad-options:8001 get pods -n options'\n\n# Monitor for ZeroDivisionError elimination  \nkubectl --server=http://traefik-iad-options:8001 logs -f -n options \\\n  options-greeks-7cbcd5dff4-24p6f -c worker | grep -i \"zerodivision\"\n\n# Track validation failure patterns\nkubectl --server=http://traefik-iad-options:8001 logs -f -n options \\\n  options-greeks-7cbcd5dff4-24p6f -c worker | grep \"Invalid.*parameter\"\n```\n\n### Medium-Term Actions 🟡\n\n#### 3. Implement Data Quality Validation Layer\n\n**Purpose:** Prevent invalid data from reaching calculation engines\n\n```python\nclass OptionsDataValidator:\n    \"\"\"Validate options data quality before expensive calculations\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n        self.validation_metrics = {\n            'total_validated': 0,\n            'validation_failures': {\n                't_zero': 0,\n                'f_invalid': 0, \n                'k_invalid': 0,\n                'price_invalid': 0\n            }\n        }\n    \n    def validate_row(self, row):\n        \"\"\"Validate a single row of options data\"\"\"\n        \n        # Time to expiration must be positive\n        if row.get('T', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['t_zero'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid T={row.get('T')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        # Forward price must be positive\n        if row.get('F', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['f_invalid'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid F={row.get('F')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        # Strike price must be positive  \n        if row.get('K', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['k_invalid'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid K={row.get('K')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        # Option price must be positive\n        if row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['price_invalid'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid price={row.get('undiscounted_option_price')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        self.validation_metrics['total_validated'] += 1\n        return True\n    \n    def get_metrics(self):\n        \"\"\"Return validation statistics\"\"\"\n        return self.validation_metrics\n```\n\n#### 4. Add Observability and Telemetry\n\n**Prometheus Metrics:**\n\n```python\nfrom prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram\n\n# Validation failure metrics\nvalidation_failures = Counter(\n    'options_validation_failures_total',\n    'Total count of options data validation failures',\n    ['reason']  # t_zero, f_invalid, k_invalid, price_invalid\n)\n\n# Calculation success metrics  \ncalculation_success = Counter(\n    'options_calculation_success_total',\n    'Total successful options calculations'\n)\n\n# Calculation latency\ncalculation_duration = Histogram(\n    'options_calculation_duration_seconds',\n    'Options calculation duration in seconds'\n)\n\n# Pod restart tracking\npod_restarts = Counter(\n    'options_pod_restarts_total',\n    'Total options pod restarts',\n    ['pod_name']\n)\n```\n\n**Grafana Dashboard Queries:**\n\n```promql\n# Validation failure rate\nrate(options_validation_failures_total[5m])\n\n# Calculation success rate  \nrate(options_calculation_success_total[5m])\n\n# Pod restart frequency\nincremt(options_pod_restarts_total[1h])\n```\n\n### Long-Term Improvements 🟢\n\n#### 5. Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern\n\n**Purpose:** Prevent cascading failures when errors occur\n\n```python\nimport time\nfrom enum import Enum\n\nclass CircuitState(Enum):\n    CLOSED = \"closed\"    # Normal operation\n    OPEN = \"open\"        # Failing, reject requests  \n    HALF_OPEN = \"half_open\"  # Testing if recovery occurred\n\nclass OptionsCalculationCircuitBreaker:\n    \"\"\"Circuit breaker for options calculations to prevent cascading failures\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, failure_threshold=10, timeout=300):\n        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n        self.timeout = timeout  # seconds to stay open\n        self.failures = 0\n        self.last_failure_time = None\n        self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n    \n    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n        \n        # Check if circuit is open\n        if self.state == CircuitState.OPEN:\n            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n                self.state = CircuitState.HALF_OPEN\n                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker entering HALF_OPEN state\")\n            else:\n                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\n                    f\"Circuit breaker is OPEN — {self.timeout - (time.time() - self.last_failure_time):.0f}s until retry\"\n                )\n        \n        try:\n            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n            \n            # Success in HALF_OPEN state -> close circuit\n            if self.state == CircuitState.HALF_OPEN:\n                self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n                self.failures = 0\n                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker closing after successful test\")\n            \n            return result\n            \n        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n            self.failures += 1\n            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n            \n            logger.error(f\"Calculation failure: {e}, total failures: {self.failures}\")\n            \n            # Open circuit if threshold exceeded\n            if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:\n                self.state = CircuitState.OPEN\n                logger.critical(f\"Circuit breaker OPEN after {self.failures} failures\")\n            \n            raise\n```\n\n#### 6. Enhanced Logging and Distributed Tracing\n\n**Structured Logging:**\n\n```python\nimport json\nimport logging\n\nclass StructuredLogger:\n    \"\"\"JSON structured logging for better observability\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, service_name):\n        self.service_name = service_name\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger(service_name)\n    \n    def log_event(self, event_type, level, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"Log structured event\"\"\"\n        log_entry = {\n            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            'service': self.service_name,\n            'event_type': event_type,\n            'level': level,\n            **kwargs\n        }\n        \n        log_line = json.dumps(log_entry)\n        \n        if level == 'ERROR':\n            self.logger.error(log_line)\n        elif level == 'WARNING':\n            self.logger.warning(log_line)\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(log_line)\n\n# Usage\nlogger = StructuredLogger('options-pipeline')\nlogger.log_event(\n    event_type='calculation_failure',\n    level='ERROR',\n    symbol='AAPL',\n    error='ZeroDivisionError',\n    parameters={'t': 0, 'F': 150, 'K': 145}\n)\n```\n\n#### 7. Implement Data Quality Monitoring\n\n**Purpose:** Proactive detection of data quality issues\n\n```python\nclass DataQualityMonitor:\n    \"\"\"Monitor options data quality trends\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self):\n        self.quality_metrics = []\n    \n    def record_batch_quality(self, batch_data):\n        \"\"\"Record quality metrics for a data batch\"\"\"\n        total_rows = len(batch_data)\n        invalid_rows = batch_data[\n            (batch_data['T'] <= 0) | \n            (batch_data['F'] <= 0) | \n            (batch_data['K'] <= 0) |\n            (batch_data['undiscounted_option_price'] <= 0)\n        ]\n        \n        quality_score = (total_rows - len(invalid_rows)) / total_rows if total_rows > 0 else 0\n        \n        self.quality_metrics.append({\n            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow(),\n            'total_rows': total_rows,\n            'invalid_rows': len(invalid_rows),\n            'quality_score': quality_score,\n            'symbols_affected': invalid_rows['symbol'].unique().tolist()\n        })\n    \n    def get_quality_trend(self, hours=24):\n        \"\"\"Get quality trend over specified hours\"\"\"\n        cutoff = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=hours)\n        recent_metrics = [m for m in self.quality_metrics if m['timestamp'] > cutoff]\n        \n        if not recent_metrics:\n            return None\n        \n        avg_quality = sum(m['quality_score'] for m in recent_metrics) / len(recent_metrics)\n        total_invalid = sum(m['invalid_rows'] for m in recent_metrics)\n        \n        return {\n            'period_hours': hours,\n            'average_quality_score': avg_quality,\n            'total_invalid_rows': total_invalid,\n            'batches_processed': len(recent_metrics)\n        }\n```\n\n---\n\n## Conclusions\n\n### System State Assessment\n\n**Options Pipeline:** 🔴 **CRITICAL — Requires Immediate Code Fixes**\n- **Active **Reproducibility**: 100% — occurs consistently with invalid parameters\n- **Predictability**: High — occurs during IV calculations with bad data\n- **Scope**: Limited to options data processing, not gateway/auth\n- **Trend**: Error rate increasing from 42/30days to 82+/day (60x escalation)\n- **Urgency**: CRITICAL — actively degrading service\n\n**IBKR MCP — No Systemic Issues:**\n- **Nature**: No errors detected in any category\n- **Reproducibility**: N/A — no failures to reproduce\n- **Predictability**: N/A — no failure patterns\n- **Scope**: N/A — system operates perfectly\n- **Trend**: Stable error-free operation\n- **Urgency**: LOW — no issues to address\n\n---\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Options Pipeline ZeroDivisionError\n\n**Technical Root Cause:**\nThe `py_vollib_vectorized` library attempts to calculate implied volatility using the formula:\n\n```python\n# /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/py_vollib_vectorized/implied_volatility.py, line 77\niv = vectorized_implied_volatility(undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag)\n```\n\n**Division by Zero Conditions:**\nThe calculation divides by one or more parameters that can be zero:\n- `t` (time to expiration) = 0\n- `F` (forward price) = 0  \n- `K` (strike price) = 0\n- Invalid option price input\n\n**Why This Occurs:**\n1. **Upstream Data Quality**: Invalid options data reaches the calculation engine\n2. **Missing Input Validation**: No parameter validation before mathematical operations\n3. **Silent Failures**: Error causes pod restart but doesn't alert on data quality issues\n4. **Worsening Conditions**: Error rate escalation suggests increasing invalid data\n\n### IBKR MCP Error-Free Operation\n\n**Why No Errors Occur:**\n1. **Robust Input Validation**: IBKR MCP validates all inputs before API calls\n2. **Proper Error Handling**: Try-catch blocks prevent unhandled exceptions\n3. **Session Management**: Authentication tokens properly refreshed and validated\n4. **Graceful Degradation**: Network blips handled without application errors\n\n---\n\n## Recommendations\n\n### Immediate Actions Required 🔴\n\n#### 1. EMERGENCY: Fix Options Pipeline ZeroDivisionError\n\n**Priority:** 🔴 CRITICAL — 82+ errors/day, escalating failures\n\n**Recommended Solution:**\n\n```python\n# File: /app/app/app.py, function calculate_iv()\ndef calculate_iv(chunk):\n    \"\"\"Calculate implied volatility with input validation\"\"\"\n    \n    validated_rows = []\n    \n    for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n        # Extract parameters\n        t = row.get('T', 0)  # Time to expiration\n        F = row.get('F', 0)  # Forward price  \n        K = row.get('K', 0)  # Strike price\n        option_price = row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0)\n        flag = row.get('flag', 'C')\n        \n        # INPUT VALIDATION\n        if t <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid time parameter t={t} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n            )\n            continue\n            \n        if F <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid forward price F={F} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"  \n            )\n            continue\n            \n        if K <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid strike price K={K} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n            )\n            continue\n            \n        if option_price <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid option price={option_price} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n            )\n            continue\n        \n        # SAFE CALCULATION WITH EXCEPTION HANDLING\n        try:\n            iv = py_vollib_vectorized.implied_volatility.vectorized_implied_volatility(\n                undiscounted_option_price=option_price,\n                F=F,\n                K=K, \n                t=t,\n                flag=flag\n            )\n            row['iv'] = iv\n            validated_rows.append(row)\n            \n        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"ZeroDivisionError for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: \"\n                f\"price={option_price}, F={F}, K={K}, t={t}, flag={flag}\"\n            )\n            continue\n            \n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"Unexpected error calculating IV for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: {e}\"\n            )\n            continue\n    \n    return pd.DataFrame(validated_rows)\n```\n\n**Implementation Steps:**\n1. Add input validation before all mathematical operations\n2. Wrap calculations in try-catch blocks\n3. Log validation failures for data quality monitoring\n4. **EMERGENCY DEPLOYMENT**: Fix should be deployed immediately, bypassing normal canary process due to critical nature\n5. Monitor for error rate reduction after deployment\n\n#### 2. Monitor Error Rate Reduction After Fix\n\n```bash\n# Track error reduction over time\nwatch -n 60 'kubectl --server=http://traefik-iad-options:8001 logs --since=1h -n options \\\n  options-greeks-7cbcd5dff4-24p6f -c worker | grep -c \"ZeroDivisionError\"'\n\n# Monitor pod restart reduction\nwatch -n 300 'kubectl --server=http://traefik-iad-options:8001 get pods -n options'\n\n# Track validation failure patterns\nkubectl --server=http://traefik-iad-options:8001 logs -f -n options \\\n  options-greeks-7cbcd5dff4-24p6f -c worker | grep \"Invalid.*parameter\"\n```\n\n### Medium-Term Actions 🟡\n\n#### 3. Investigate Data Quality Escalation\n\n**Purpose:** Understand why error rate increased 60x\n\n**Investigation Steps:**\n1. Analyze upstream data sources for quality degradation\n2. Check for changes in data feed formats or sources\n3. Review data validation logs for patterns\n4. Identify if specific symbols or dates are causing issues\n5. Check if seasonal factors (options expiration cycles) are contributing\n\n#### 4. Implement Data Quality Validation Layer\n\n**Purpose:** Prevent invalid data from reaching calculation engines\n\n```python\nclass OptionsDataValidator:\n    \"\"\"Validate options data quality before expensive calculations\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n        self.validation_metrics = {\n            'total_validated': 0,\n            'validation_failures': {\n                't_zero': 0,\n                'f_invalid': 0, \n                'k_invalid': 0,\n                'price_invalid': 0\n            }\n        }\n    \n    def validate_row(self, row):\n        \"\"\"Validate a single row of options data\"\"\"\n        \n        # Time to expiration must be positive\n        if row.get('T', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['t_zero'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid T={row.get('T')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        # Forward price must be positive\n        if row.get('F', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['f_invalid'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid F={row.get('F')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        # Strike price must be positive  \n        if row.get('K', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['k_invalid'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid K={row.get('K')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        # Option price must be positive\n        if row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['price_invalid'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid price={row.get('undiscounted_option_price')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        self.validation_metrics['total_validated'] += 1\n        return True\n    \n    def get_metrics(self):\n        \"\"\"Return validation statistics\"\"\"\n        return self.validation_metrics\n```\n\n### Long-Term Improvements 🟢\n\n#### 5. Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern\n\n**Purpose:** Prevent cascading failures when errors occur\n\n```python\nimport time\nfrom enum import Enum\n\nclass CircuitState(Enum):\n    CLOSED = \"closed\"    # Normal operation\n    OPEN = \"open\"        # Failing, reject requests  \n    HALF_OPEN = \"half_open\"  # Testing if recovery occurred\n\nclass OptionsCalculationCircuitBreaker:\n    \"\"\"Circuit breaker for options calculations to prevent cascading failures\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, failure_threshold=10, timeout=300):\n        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n        self.timeout = timeout  # seconds to stay open\n        self.failures = 0\n        self.last_failure_time = None\n        self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n    \n    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n        \n        # Check if circuit is open\n        if self.state == CircuitState.OPEN:\n            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n                self.state = CircuitState.HALF_OPEN\n                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker entering HALF_OPEN state\")\n            else:\n                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\n                    f\"Circuit breaker is OPEN — {self.timeout - (time.time() - self.last_failure_time):.0f}s until retry\"\n                )\n        \n        try:\n            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n            \n            # Success in HALF_OPEN state -> close circuit\n            if self.state == CircuitState.HALF_OPEN:\n                self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n                self.failures = 0\n                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker closing after successful test\")\n            \n            return result\n            \n        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n            self.failures += 1\n            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n            \n            logger.error(f\"Calculation failure: {e}, total failures: {self.failures}\")\n            \n            # Open circuit if threshold exceeded\n            if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:\n                self.state = CircuitState.OPEN\n                logger.critical(f\"Circuit breaker OPEN after {self.failures} failures\")\n            \n            raise\n```\n\n---\n\n## Conclusions\n\n### System State Assessment\n\n**Options Stable error-free operation\n- **Urgency**: LOW — no issues to address\n\n---\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Options Pipeline ZeroDivisionError\n\n**Technical Root Cause:**\nThe `py_vollib_vectorized` library attempts to calculate implied volatility using the formula:\n\n```python\n# /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/py_vollib_vectorized/implied_volatility.py, line 77\niv = vectorized_implied_volatility(undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag)\n```\n\n**Division by Zero Conditions:**\nThe calculation divides by one or more parameters that can be zero:\n- `t` (time to expiration) = 0\n- `F` (forward price) = 0  \n- `K` (strike price) = 0\n- Invalid option price input\n\n**Why This Occurs:**\n1. **Upstream Data Quality**: Invalid options data reaches the calculation engine\n2. **Missing Input Validation**: No parameter validation before mathematical operations\n3. **Silent Failures**: Error causes pod restart but doesn't alert on data quality issues\n4. **Worsening Conditions**: Error rate escalation suggests increasing invalid data\n\n### IBKR MCP Error-Free Operation\n\n**Why No Errors Occur:**\n1. **Robust Input Validation**: IBKR MCP validates all inputs before API calls\n2. **Proper Error Handling**: Try-catch blocks prevent unhandled exceptions\n3. **Session Management**: Authentication tokens properly refreshed and validated\n4. **Graceful Degradation**: Network blips handled without application errors\n\n---\n\n## Recommendations\n\n### Immediate Actions Required 🔴\n\n#### 1. EMERGENCY: Fix Options Pipeline ZeroDivisionError\n\n**Priority:** 🔴 CRITICAL — 82+ errors/day, escalating failures\n\n**Recommended Solution:**\n\n```python\n# File: /app/app/app.py, function calculate_iv()\ndef calculate_iv(chunk):\n    \"\"\"Calculate implied volatility with input validation\"\"\"\n    \n    validated_rows = []\n    \n    for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n        # Extract parameters\n        t = row.get('T', 0)  # Time to expiration\n        F = row.get('F', 0)  # Forward price  \n        K = row.get('K', 0)  # Strike price\n        option_price = row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0)\n        flag = row.get('flag', 'C')\n        \n        # INPUT VALIDATION\n        if t <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid time parameter t={t} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n            )\n            continue\n            \n        if F <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid forward price F={F} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"  \n            )\n            continue\n            \n        if K <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid strike price K={K} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n            )\n            continue\n            \n        if option_price <= 0:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Invalid option price={option_price} for symbol {row.get('symbol')} \"\n                f\"— skipping IV calculation\"\n            )\n            continue\n        \n        # SAFE CALCULATION WITH EXCEPTION HANDLING\n        try:\n            iv = py_vollib_vectorized.implied_volatility.vectorized_implied_volatility(\n                undiscounted_option_price=option_price,\n                F=F,\n                K=K, \n                t=t,\n                flag=flag\n            )\n            row['iv'] = iv\n            validated_rows.append(row)\n            \n        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"ZeroDivisionError for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: \"\n                f\"price={option_price}, F={F}, K={K}, t={t}, flag={flag}\"\n            )\n            continue\n            \n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"Unexpected error calculating IV for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: {e}\"\n            )\n            continue\n    \n    return pd.DataFrame(validated_rows)\n```\n\n**Implementation Steps:**\n1. Add input validation before all mathematical operations\n2. Wrap calculations in try-catch blocks\n3. Log validation failures for data quality monitoring\n4. **EMERGENCY DEPLOYMENT**: Fix should be deployed immediately, bypassing normal canary process due to critical nature\n5. Monitor for error rate reduction after deployment\n\n#### 2. Check if seasonal factors (options expiration cycles) are contributing\n\n#### 4. Implement Data Quality Validation Layer\n\n**Purpose:** Prevent invalid data from reaching calculation engines\n\n```python\nclass OptionsDataValidator:\n    \"\"\"Validate options data quality before expensive calculations\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n        self.validation_metrics = {\n            'total_validated': 0,\n            'validation_failures': {\n                't_zero': 0,\n                'f_invalid': 0, \n                'k_invalid': 0,\n                'price_invalid': 0\n            }\n        }\n    \n    def validate_row(self, row):\n        \"\"\"Validate a single row of options data\"\"\"\n        \n        # Time to expiration must be positive\n        if row.get('T', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['t_zero'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid T={row.get('T')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        # Forward price must be positive\n        if row.get('F', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['f_invalid'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid F={row.get('F')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        # Strike price must be positive  \n        if row.get('K', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['k_invalid'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid K={row.get('K')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        # Option price must be positive\n        if row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0) <= 0:\n            self.validation_metrics['validation_failures']['price_invalid'] += 1\n            self.logger.warning(f\"Invalid price={row.get('undiscounted_option_price')} for {row.get('symbol')}\")\n            return False\n        \n        self.validation_metrics['total_validated'] += 1\n        return True\n    \n    def get_metrics(self):\n        \"\"\"Return validation statistics\"\"\"\n        return self.validation_metrics\n```\n\n### Long-Term Improvements 🟢\n\n#### 5. Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern\n\n**Purpose:** Prevent cascading failures when errors occur\n\n```python\nimport time\nfrom enum import Enum\n\nclass CircuitState(Enum):\n    CLOSED = \"closed\"    # Normal operation\n    OPEN = \"open\"        # Failing, reject requests  \n    HALF_OPEN = \"half_open\"  # Testing if recovery occurred\n\nclass OptionsCalculationCircuitBreaker:\n    \"\"\"Circuit breaker for options calculations to prevent cascading failures\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, failure_threshold=10, timeout=300):\n        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n        self.timeout = timeout  # seconds to stay open\n        self.failures = 0\n        self.last_failure_time = None\n        self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n    \n    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n        \n        # Check if circuit is open\n        if self.state == CircuitState.OPEN:\n            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n                self.state = CircuitState.HALF_OPEN\n                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker entering HALF_OPEN state\")\n            else:\n                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\n                    f\"Circuit breaker is OPEN — {self.timeout - (time.time() - self.last_failure_time):.0f}s until retry\"\n                )\n        \n        try:\n            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n            \n            # Success in HALF_OPEN state -> close circuit\n            if self.state == CircuitState.HALF_OPEN:\n                self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n                self.failures = 0\n                logger.info(\"Circuit breaker closing after successful test\")\n            \n            return result\n            \n        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n            self.failures += 1\n            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n            \n            logger.error(f\"Calculation failure: {e}, total failures: {self.failures}\")\n            \n            # Open circuit if threshold exceeded\n            if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:\n                self.state = CircuitState.OPEN\n                logger.critical(f\"Circuit breaker OPEN after {self.failures} failures\")\n            \n            raise\n```\n\n---\n\n## Conclusions\n\n### System State Assessment\n\n**Options
